Distractions while using Wi-Fi fall into two main categories: the digital content delivered through the connection and the physical environment affecting the signal itself.
Digital Distractions
When you have a stable Wi-Fi connection, the sheer volume of accessible content becomes the primary source of distraction:
Social Media & Messaging: Constant notifications from platforms like Instagram, Facebook, and WhatsApp.
"Tab Explosion": Having too many open browser tabs leads to constant "task-switching," which can drop your efficiency by up to 40%.
Infinite Content Loops: Rabbit holes like YouTube videos, clickbait headlines, and online games
